mechanism_narrative: Sustained real GDP growth, as measured by GDPC1, signals expanding economic activity and rising corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter. This relationship is fundamental and time-invariant.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 quarterly growth is positive but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of quarters over a 10-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates labor market deterioration, reducing consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by at least 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period
failure_condition: If in instances triggered by the condition, S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of cases over a 5-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When both inflation (CPIAUCSL) is elevated and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is rising, the central bank is tightening policy, which compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced liquidity. The interaction of high inflation and rising rates amplifies the negative effect on equity returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change > 3% and FEDFUNDS increased in the last two months
failure_condition: If the joint condition triggers but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of events over any 3-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: During the late-cycle expansion phase, decelerating industrial production (INDPRO) growth signals that the economy is approaching a peak and will soon contract, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as investors anticipate recession.
failure_condition: If during periods identified as late-cycle expansion (e.g., based on yield curve and capacity utilization), INDPRO deceleration is followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of cases, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in payroll employment (PAYEMS) reflects a strong labor market that supports consumer income and spending, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS monthly growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of months over a 5-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in industrial production (INDPRO) over two consecutive months is a leading indicator of economic contraction, which reduces corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change negative for two consecutive months
failure_condition: If triggered events are followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases over a 10-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate broad-based weakening in consumer and investment demand, foreshadowing an economic downturn that depresses equity prices over the next month.
candidate_trigger: Both RSAFS and HOUST decline month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: If the joint trigger is followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 40% of instances over a 5-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime where CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 3%, any increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals further tightening that will slow the economy and compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 25 basis points in a month
failure_condition: If the trigger occurs while CPI inflation > 3% but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of events over any 3-year period, reject.
